{{Infobox character
| name = Elderspawn
| appears_in = The Elder Empire: Sea, The Elder Empire: Shadow
| abilities = command others, imitate human voices
| affiliations = Children of Nakothi
| introduced = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
}}

''See also: [[Elderspawn Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Elderspawn are the diverse and often monstrous servants of the [[Great Elders]]. They vary significantly in biology, appearance, and intelligence, ranging from animalistic creatures with little intellect to highly coordinated, intelligent beings. Some species are described as ten-legged arachnid [[Inquisitors]] with numerous eyes, while others manifest as giant worms, amphibious hordes, shadow-men, or even flatworms with crocodile-like jaws. Their physical forms are highly inconsistent; some possess multiple eyes on stalks, while others have gills, tentacles, or wings. Their blood is often a distinct purple color. The role and behavior of the Elderspawn depend on their specific origin. While some are viewed as mere animals or parasites that feed on human emotions like vengeance, others act with precise coordination and a sense of purpose. They are often used by the [[Great Elders]] to influence or corrupt humanity, acting as tools for their masters' agendas. Some Elderspawn are capable of communicating through speech or commands, and they can be found in various environments, from the depths of the ocean to urban settings. While some are malicious and soul-devouring, others are simply alien and strange without being inherently hostile. They are frequently hunted by specialized groups like the [[Blackwatch]], who are trained to combat these creatures.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Elderspawn are giant, alien monsters with unfathomable faces that may lack heads, hearts, spines, or limbs. These creatures possess an awareness that can be read by [[Calder]] and act as guides for [[Navigators]] crossing the [[Aion]], though they can also attack gates or tear open prisons. While some Elderspawn may be summoned through [[Intent]] or arrive during ritual sequences, they can be immobilized by contact with [[Awakened iron]]. Despite their strange biology and mentality, which includes having six black eyes and gills, they are not necessarily malicious, though an army of them can crawl across an [[island]]. These beings do not experience tiredness in the human sense, and their volume serves as a measure for loudness.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|An [[Elderspawn]] is a clawed creature that [[Calder]] had to nail to the inside of his hull.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c1">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that may not get tired in the human sense.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The volume of [[Elderspawn]] is a measure for loudness.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Elderspawn]] is an entity with awareness that can be read by [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] act as guides for the [[Navigators]] to cross the [[Aion]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Sleepless]] might send an [[Elderspawn]] killer to eliminate a witness.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Some [[Elderspawn]] lack heads, hearts, spines, or limbs.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c10">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|An [[Elderspawn]] may have developed the ability to command others.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c11">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Andel]] did not want a blade that remembered being inside an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are immobilized when they make contact with [[Awakened iron]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are monsters that attack the gates.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The ruler of the [[Empire]] is described as an Elderspawned excuse for a ruler.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c15">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|An [[Elderspawn]] is a giant creature.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c18">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that can bother ships unless an [[Elder]] is chained to them.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that can be strange or alien in biology and mentality, but not necessarily malicious.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] may arrive during a ritual sequence.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are beings that can be summoned through the application of [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Elderspawn]] is a creature that can tear open the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are monsters that include the [[Children of Nakothi]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Elderspawn]] has six black eyes and gills.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[island]] is crawling with an army of [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Emperor]] is compared to [[Elderspawn]] due to his detachment from humanity.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures with unfathomable faces.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c27">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Elderspawn are winged, dark green creatures with tentacles writhing over their mouths that roving street gangs in poor quarters often resemble. These entities can be used in ship construction to help captains navigate the hazards of the deep [[Aion]], and a giant specimen was once chained beneath the [[Navigator]]'s ship. While they are difficult to kill, sometimes requiring a second blade, [[Meia]] has successfully slain a giant Elderspawn using only her bare hands. During conflicts on the [[island]], [[Lucan]] fought against them, though every Elderspawn eventually vanished after being swept away by [[Bastion's Veil]]. [[Calder Marten]] maintains captive Elderspawn and utilizes an orange-and-black [[sword]] that possesses a specific effect on the creatures.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] are entities that roving bands of street gangs resemble in the poorer quarters.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c4">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The prisoner was once mistaken for [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c5">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A giant [[Elderspawn]] is tied to the hull of the [[Navigator]]'s ship.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c6">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] are chained beneath the ship.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c8">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] tore apart the man killed by [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c10">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Using parts of [[Elderspawn]] in a ship's construction allows it to guide the captain around hazards of the deep [[Aion]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] were not seen on the way to the [[island]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|An [[Elderspawn]] was likely the messenger sent by the [[Sleepless]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c15">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Calder Marten]] has captive [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that a second blade might be needed to kill.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|An [[Elderspawn]] is a creature with dark green skin and small wings.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c23">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Calder Marten]]'s orange-and-black [[sword]] has an effect on [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c25">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that can flare wings and have tentacles writhing over their mouths.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Lucan]] fought against the [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Every [[Elderspawn]] on the [[island]] has vanished.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that can be swept off the [[Island]] by [[Bastion's Veil]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Meia]] has killed a giant [[Elderspawn]] with her bare hands.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c27">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Characterized by a perpetual state of malice and burning frustration, these ten-legged entities possess spear-sharp limbs and chitin that ranges from slick purple to dark blue. While they were once used as messengers, their presence rose globally during the [[Long Mourning]], and they have since been sighted in locations such as [[Dylia]], [[Vandenyas]], the Nire, and the [[Capital]]. These creatures, which resemble wild lions and can imitate human voices, are capable of emptying entire villages or spreading diseases that transform people into monsters. Though some variations like the low-intelligence [[Shuffles]] exist, these hordes are known to kneel and have been used to feed prisoners or were even kept as pets by [[Calder]]. During violent encounters in [[the Emperor]]'s courtyard, fleshy Elderspawn have been known to fight and leave behind gore.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are used as messengers.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Reading]] an [[Elderspawn]] directly can cause madness.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] rose all over the world during the [[Long Mourning]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are in the courtyard outside the [[Emperor's quarters]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|An [[Elderspawn]] invasion spread a disease that turned people into monsters.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c9">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] gore was found in the courtyard.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] exist in a perpetual state of malice and burning frustration.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] have been sighted in [[Dylia]], [[Vandenyas]], the Nire, and the [[Capital]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are entities capable of emptying villages.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] have chitin that is dark blue or slick purple.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are described similarly to wild lions.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are entities that prisoners are fed to.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] have ten spider-legs.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] have spear-sharp legs.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that screech on the shore of [[Silverreach]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Fleshy [[Elderspawn]] fought in the courtyard of [[the Emperor]]'s chambers.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] can imitate human voices.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c17">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] has a pet [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] include [[Shuffles]], who have little intelligence more than animals.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]] are hordes that can kneel.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23" />}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|These hideous creatures, which are more repulsive than any [[Kameira]], possess dark purple or near-black hairy bodies and ten legs. Characterized by a shifting, skittering movement, they feature eyes situated on stalks upon their backs and can even borrow a person's face. During a battle, [[Kerian]] managed to sweep an army of these entities from the shores by collapsing the [[Veil]]. They are known for their sickening purple blood and death-screams that sound like a blend of a whistling teakettle and a screaming horse, which produce floating green lights upon their passing. While they showed no concern for humans and even constructed a bronze door within a tunnel, the Guard leader eventually identified [[Darius]] as one of these beings during a period when they were being cleansed from the [[Capital]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The deaths of [[Elderspawn]] produce floating green lights.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c2">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kerian]] swept an army of [[Elderspawn]] from the shores by collapsing the [[Veil]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|There was a battle with the [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c4">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The girls survived a meeting with an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c6">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|There is a possibility of a fight with [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c7">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The Guard leader identifies [[Darius]] as an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]] created the bronze door in the tunnel.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|An [[Elderspawn]] is a creature that moves with a shifting, skittering movement.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Elderspawn]] creature is a ten-legged spider-like entity with a dark purple, almost black, hairy body.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Elderspawn]] creature has eyes on stalks positioned on its back.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that do not care about humans.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]] have sickening purple blood and death-screams that sound like a blend of a screaming horse and a whistling teakettle.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]] are being cleansed from the [[Capital]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|An [[Elderspawn]] is a creature more hideous than any [[Kameira]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c18">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]] are beings that can borrow a person's face.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c19">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Created by [[Othaghor]], these lime-green creatures serve the [[Elders]] and act as messengers that translate the voices of the [[Sleepless]] cabal. Found around the [[Capital]], these entities possess six yellow eyes, a dozen webbed feet, stingers, and extendible jaws filled with crocodile teeth. While some Elderspawn served [[Jerri]] by transporting food and waste during her imprisonment, others appeared as giant fanged fish or aquatic masses seething in the surf. Because they can affect the minds and bodies of victims like [[Mister Goss]], people use silver rings for protection, and certain [[Pilgrims]] develop a holy zeal to eradicate them. Ultimately, they are the specific targets for which the [[Awakened sword]] was designed, even as pieces of their corpses began stitching themselves back together alongside human remains.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] messengers translate the voices of the [[Sleepless]] cabal.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c5">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] report back to the [[Sleepless leaders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Estyr]] identified something or someone as an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Silver rings]] are used to ward off [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c9">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] may be encircling the tower.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c10">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Giant fanged [[Elderspawn]] are in the surf.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c12">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are lime-green creatures with six yellow eyes and a dozen webbed amphibian feet.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] have stingers.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are giant flatworms with extendible jaws and crocodile teeth.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Elderspawn]] have affected [[Mister Goss]]'s mind and body.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] mentions having seen [[Elderspawn]] in his speech.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c15">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are creations of [[Othaghor]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c16">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Some [[Pilgrims]] gain a holy zeal toward the eradication of [[Elderspawn]] due to the [[Awakening]] process.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] served [[Jerri]] by dragging food and disposing of waste during her imprisonment.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are servants of [[Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are creatures that exist around the [[Capital]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Ordinary people in the [[Capital]] invested objects to protect them from [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c21">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|A mass of aquatic [[Elderspawn]] seethed beneath the surface.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are fanged fish with clawed, stubby legs and arms.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The water was dark with [[Elderspawn]] corpses.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] snaps [[Elderspawn]] as he marches forward.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Elderspawn]] are entities that the [[Awakened sword]] was designed to be used against.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Pieces of [[Elderspawn]] and human corpses were stitching themselves together.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The Elderspawn serve as a method for the [[Creeping Shadow]] to exert influence over the world, with some individuals sharing a hive-like mind. Following a global uprising on the night of [[the Emperor]]'s death, these creatures were even known to drive out certain [[Elder]] tombs. While they are unable to pass through the mist of the [[Bastion]]’s [[Veil]] and can be torched by [[Loreli]]'s [[Beacon]], they remain a persistent threat. They have been observed pooling at [[Darius Allbright]]'s feet, swarming decks, and inhabiting the waters surrounding the [[Navigators]]. These beings of flesh and bone are capable of crawling up railings, and their presence has been signaled by the sight of distant armies.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Some [[Elderspawn]] share a mind like a hive.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c5">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Estyr]] uses the term '[[Elderspawn]]' as a declaration of hatred and fury.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c6">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Allbright]] suggests flushing out the [[Elderspawn]] from the [[Imperial Palace]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Elderspawn]] cannot pass through the [[Bastion]]’s [[Veil]] mist.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c13">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|There was an uprising of [[Elderspawn]] around the world on the night of [[the Emperor]]’s death.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Loreli]]'s [[Beacon]] light torches [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c18">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Crafting [[Elderspawn]] is a way the [[Creeping Shadow]] can influence the world.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Some [[Elder]] tombs were driven out by [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Elderspawn]] pooled around [[Darius Allbright]]'s feet and clung to walls.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c23">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|An army of [[Elderspawn]] was seen in the distance.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Elderspawn]] are in the water around the [[Navigators]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Elderspawn]] were on the deck.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Elderspawn]] of flesh and bone can crawl up railings.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|In volume no less than the Elderspawn, he announced, “Danger! All hands on deck!<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|And the charges worked. After that, Foster went from calling Petal a “waste of bilge-space” to “genius.” Calder knew he wouldn’t have to bother his pet Elderspawn.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|So-called “street gangs” had been the darlings of the newssheets for five years, haunting the poorer quarters like roving bands of Elderspawn.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c4" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|“Sweeps all lesser Elderspawn off the Island.”<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|“Elderspawn! Seize him for questioning!”<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Elders and Elderspawn]]
